provenance.worktree_note: The lane specs and manager board state that court_calibration.py and court_calibration_metric15.py belong to another lane and may carry uncommitted edits. Initial file-scoped git diff, git diff --cached, and git status checks showed no worktree/index delta for either file; both were nevertheless treated as read-only. During final validation another lane's unstaged court_calibration_metric15.py edit became visible (coordinate helpers, extrinsics inversion, and coordinate-contract payload); court_calibration.py remained clean. This consult did not modify either file. HEAD also advanced concurrently from 0e97c09fe1218b1a754222f879e40ae996e24657 to a57476a39ffbdae6d137e958c6c8d81bf8e3c04f through unrelated commits.

default_pipeline_flow.runner: YOLO26m plus BoT-SORT/ReID or precomputed tracks
default_pipeline_flow.coordinate_note: Initial bbox-bottom-center projection uses the calibration homography into WORLD_XY_HOMOGRAPHY_M.
Each compensated M maps current-frame pixels into the calibration-reference frame; artifact remains preview/not gate verified.

Raw pixels are optionally undistorted when the calibration declares an undistorted-pixel homography; camera-motion M is applied before inverse-homography projection; covariance is propagated from pixel sigma through the homography Jacobian.

default_pipeline_flow.runner: WASB plus manual-corner in/out plus calibrated arc reconstruction
default_pipeline_flow.coordinate_note: The current stage-6 in/out path independently rebuilds a native-pixel homography from court_corners.json; it does not consume court_calibration.json. The 3D arc path does consume court_calibration.json and emits court/world XYZ.
Track world_xy is the horizontal root anchor; BODY samples and meshes are emitted in court_Z0 world space. Camera-motion correction is limited to pixel-to-world grounding inputs.
Player, BODY, ball and paddle presentation is assembled in court_Z0; zone facts use metric track_world_xy against template court_zones.

calibration_paths.name: manual_four_corner
calibration_paths.camera_model: four planar corners, homography plus solvePnP-oriented artifact; fallback intrinsics may be estimated from declared corners
calibration_paths.trust: requires human-reviewed corners for product verification; reprojection of the four points is not independent accuracy proof

calibration_paths.name: arkit_metric_keypoints
calibration_paths.camera_model: ARKit intrinsics, pose and floor plane plus court keypoints
calibration_paths.trust: future-facing no-tap path remains fail-closed by automatic evidence policy

calibration_paths.name: reviewed_metric15
calibration_paths.camera_model: fx, fy, k1, k2 plus pose from reviewed 15-point evidence; emitted homography declares its pixel convention
calibration_paths.trust: reviewed input and trusted source, but still not a CAL promotion without the owner-viewpoint gate

calibration_paths.name: profile_reuse
calibration_paths.solve: frozen profile calibration verified by fingerprint, paint color and four outer lines
calibration_paths.trust: implemented library and tests, not wired into process_video.py or orchestrator.py

calibration_paths.name: auto_find_preview
calibration_paths.solve: five sampled frames -> court_line_keypoints -> best-confidence four corners -> manual runner
calibration_paths.trust: model_estimated preview only; poor capture quality reasons retained; tracking gets a 2 m margin

calibration_paths.name: proposal_preview
calibration_paths.solve: line bank, regulation hypotheses, optional detector-v2/net-anchor proposals
calibration_paths.trust: writes review/correction artifacts and hard-stops; never becomes court_calibration.json automatically

downstream_consumers.coordinates: raw or undistorted image pixels -> calibration-reference pixels -> court/world XY metres
downstream_consumers.precision_dependency: direct; homography bias moves every player anchor and can change on/off-court filtering

downstream_consumers.coordinates: camera-relative BODY offsets anchored to track_world_xy and emitted in court_Z0
downstream_consumers.precision_dependency: direct horizontally; placement error becomes BODY root/feet placement error

downstream_consumers.coordinates: image pixels projected by the calibrated camera into court/world XYZ
downstream_consumers.precision_dependency: direct for arc scale, bounce position, net clearance and fail-closed spatial sanity

downstream_consumers.coordinates: native ball pixels -> separately rebuilt manual-corner court-plane XY
downstream_consumers.precision_dependency: separate seam today; improvements to court_calibration.json do not automatically reach stage-6 in/out until integration unifies the authority
direct for heatmaps, kitchen proximity, recovery and court-coverage facts

refinement_and_temporal_hooks.why_noop_or_worse: Returns raw predictions when fewer than 8 channels agree within 30 px; failed cross-source models therefore usually no-op. It fits all named points using world x/y only, so the three net-top points lose their non-planar z and can worsen results. Four-point subset enumeration also ignores confidence and line evidence.
run_guarded_line_refinement returns initial_h unchanged and score_homography_support returns four zeros; the wrapper explicitly records optimizer_not_wired.

refinement_and_temporal_hooks.status: real offline diagnostic implementation, not default wired
refinement_and_temporal_hooks.why_noop_or_worse: Contains LM homography, point+line, radial-distortion and metric-plane fits, but consumes reviewed points/line observations and has no default-pipeline integration or independent accept guard. Full intrinsics from one plane is explicitly marked unidentifiable/diagnostic-only.
Can robustly test added named correspondences, but the proposal solver never writes court_calibration.json and its residual is self-consistency against the same correspondences.

Produces current-pixel-to-reference transforms and placement/worldhmr can consume them, but it does not emit per-frame calibrated camera parameters and BALL 3D does not consume camera_motion.json. A single bad reference or drift can therefore remain systemic.
Has useful 4.8 px median/12.3 px p95/three-line reuse guards but no process_video/orchestrator caller.

Scores detected segments against a fixed solved calibration but does not optimize the calibration; thresholded nearest-line selection can be circular if reused as an objective without fixed visibility and clutter competition.
